title: A recursive core for partition function form games
abstract: We present a well-defined generalisation of the core to coalitional games with externalities, where the value of a deviation is given by an endogenous response, the solution (if nonempty: the core) of the residual game.
summary_en: Standard cooperative game theory defines the "core" — the set of allocations no coalition can improve on by leaving — but the concept is fragile when coalitions affect each other (externalities). This paper introduces a recursive core that handles externalities by defining a deviating coalition's value through an endogenous response: whatever remains is itself a game, solved by the core if it's nonempty. A clean generalisation that makes the core applicable to partition-function-form games. Useful for thinking about international-treaty coalitions, cartel formation, and any setting where groups affect each other.
policy_relevance: Foundational for modelling international-coalition formation (climate agreements, trade blocs, EU policy coordination). Relevant to Hungarian EU-negotiation strategy analysis. Caveat: highly theoretical; specific policy applications require concrete parameterisation.
